title: CLAUDE.md - selmaR Package
text: Connection object pattern — selma_connect() returns selma_connection S3 object storing base_url, token, api_version. Passed fetch functions. Similar DBI connections: API version connection — api_version ("v2" "v3") lives connection object, per-request parameter. SELMA instance runs one version. Default NULL (auto-detect probing API connect time). Version-specific config (path prefix, member key, auth endpoint) lives .api_config R/api_version.R. Version config registry — version-specific constants .api_config, never hardcoded inline: Schema-driven field handling — R/sysdata.rda contains .selma_schemas, nested list expected fields ID column names per entity per API version, generated build time data-raw/openapi/. standardize_selma_data() function uses instead hardcoded switch(). Soft validation: warn (error) expected fields missing, SELMA API changes produce actionable messages, silent wrong data. Fail-loud member extraction — Never page_data[["hydra:member"]] inline. Always use extract_members(page_data, api_version) : tries version-appropriate key, falls back version’s key warning, errors immediately (response keys listed) neither found. field translation layer — v3 returns different field names v2 (e.g. first_name vs forename). package surfaces API returns. Breaking field name changes documented vignettes/api-v3-migration.Rmd, papered renaming logic. Environment variable auth — Support SELMA_BASE_URL, SELMA_EMAIL, SELMA_PASSWORD env vars defaults (like R API packages). Tidy output — functions return tibbles clean_names() (snake_case). Hydra JSON-LD artifacts user-facing output. Optional caching — Functions accept cache = TRUE/FALSE cache_dir parameters. Cache uses RDS files configurable TTL. Caching opt-, default. Progress reporting — Support progressr long-running fetches (thousands pages). Use p = NULL default pattern backward compatibility. hardcoded org details — base_url, credentials, org-specific mappings must parameters, never constants. Minimal dependencies — httr2, jsonlite, tibble, janitor, cli, rlang. Suggest (require) progressr. add rapiclient — handle Hydra pagination pins httr dependency.
code: con <- selma_connect(base_url = "https://myorg.selma.co.nz/",                      email = "api@selma.co.nz",                      password = "secret")   # api_version auto-detected students <- selma_students(con) .api_config <- list(   v2 = list(path_prefix = "app/", member_key = "hydra:member", total_key = "hydra:totalItems", ...),   v3 = list(path_prefix = "api/", member_key = "member",       total_key = "totalItems",      ...) )

title: Moving to SELMA API v3 — What Changes and What Gets Better
text: selmaR handles nearly v2→v3 differences automatically. cases need reconnect: need update code: column names downstream scripts. v3 renamed fields (e.g. forename → first_name, enrstatus → enrolment_status). field name change tables end article reference .
code: # Re-run selma_connect() after your SELMA instance upgrades. # Version is auto-detected — no other changes needed for basic fetching. con <- selma_connect() con$api_version  # confirms "v3"

title: Moving to SELMA API v3 — What Changes and What Gets Better
text: v2 limited server-side filtering. wanted enrolments one student, fetch whole enrolments table filter R. v3 adds proper filter parameters key endpoints. can ask SELMA server return records need: Filtered calls bypass cache automatically (cached dataset full unfiltered table), risk filtered call overwriting full cached data.
code: # Instead of fetching 50,000 enrolment records and filtering in R... all_enrs <- selma_enrolments(con) student_enrs <- dplyr::filter(all_enrs, student_id == 456)  # ...just fetch what you need directly: student_enrs <- selma_enrolments(con, filter = list(student = "456"))  # Same for intakes: upcoming <- selma_intakes(con, filter = list(`start_date[after]` = "2025-01-01"))  # And components for a specific enrolment: comps <- selma_components(con, filter = list(enrolment = "456"))

what: 2. id is always the primary key — no surprises
title: Moving to SELMA API v3 — What Changes and What Gets Better
text: v2 API responses wrapped records Hydra JSON-LD, included @id field containing record’s IRI (e.g. "/app/students/42"). combined integer primary key also called id, created naming collision forced messy workaround (id_2). selmaR resolves cleanly: @id, @type, @context stripped every response processing. result id every tibble receive always integer primary key — clean, predictable, joinable without casting.
code: students <- selma_students(con) # id is always the integer primary key # no id_2, no @id artifacts head(students$id) #> [1] 1 2 3 4 5 6

what: 3. Foreign keys tell you exactly what they point to
title: Moving to SELMA API v3 — What Changes and What Gets Better
text: v2, foreign keys bare integers — student_id = 42 — hint referred . v3, foreign keys arrive IRI references: selmaR strips trailing segment ("42", "10") stores character columns. get ID value directly, column name tells entity type. one practical consequence type cast joining: convenience helpers (selma_join_students(), selma_join_intakes(), etc.) handle cast .
code: "student": "/api/students/42", "intake":  "/api/intakes/10" library(dplyr)  # id is integer; student (FK) is character after IRI stripping enrolments |>   mutate(student_id = as.integer(student)) |>   left_join(students, by = c("student_id" = "id"))

title: Moving to SELMA API v3 — What Changes and What Gets Better
text: v2 field names inherited SELMA database inconsistent — mix camelCase, PascalCase, abbreviated names (enrstartdate, ThirdPartyID1, compenrid). v3 standardises snake_case throughout. Combined clean_names() applied read time, every tibble receive clean, predictable column names:
code: # v2                    → v3 # enrstartdate          → start_date # enrstatus             → enrolment_status # compenrid             → id # forename              → first_name # ThirdPartyID1         → other_id_1

title: EFTS Pro-Rata Reporting
text: EFTS (Equivalent Full-Time Student) standard funding metric tertiary education New Zealand. selmaR includes selma_efts_report() pro-rate component’s EFTS across calendar months — replicating SELMA’s built-funding report.

title: EFTS Pro-Rata Reporting
text: component: Calculate total_days = end_date - start_date + 1 calendar month, calculate overlap days component’s date range month Monthly EFTS = (overlap_days / total_days) * component_efts distributes component’s EFTS across months proportionally much duration falls within month.

title: EFTS Pro-Rata Reporting
text: result contains:
code: library(selmaR)  con <- selma_connect() components <- selma_components(con)  # Single year report_2025 <- selma_efts_report(components, year = 2025)

title: EFTS Pro-Rata Reporting
text: default, selma_efts_report(): Includes statuses: C, FC, FI, WR, WS (funded) Excludes international fee-paying (source “02”) Excludes cross-credited enrolments (components summing zero EFTS) Customise :
code: # Only confirmed and completed report <- selma_efts_report(   components,   year = 2025,   funded_statuses = c("C", "FC") )  # Include international students report <- selma_efts_report(   components,   year = 2025,   exclude_international = FALSE )

title: Getting Started with selmaR
text: Use convenience join functions — need remember join keys: Note joining v3 data: v3, foreign key columns like student intake contain character IDs (e.g. "42"), primary key id columns integers. Use .integer() .character() align types joining:
code: library(dplyr)  # Build a full student pipeline in one line pipeline <- selma_student_pipeline(enrolments, students, intakes)  # Or use individual join helpers selma_join_students(enrolments, students)     # enrolments + student details selma_join_intakes(enrolments, intakes)       # enrolments + intake dates selma_join_components(components, enrolments) # components + enrolment info selma_join_programmes(intakes, programmes)    # intakes + programme names selma_join_notes(notes, students)             # notes + student details selma_join_addresses(addresses, students)     # addresses + student details selma_join_classes(classes, campuses)         # classes + campus locations selma_join_attempts(attempts, components)     # attempts + component details  # Full component pipeline: components + enrolments + students + intakes full <- selma_component_pipeline(components, enrolments, students, intakes) enrolments |>   dplyr::mutate(student_id = as.integer(student)) |>   dplyr::left_join(students, by = c("student_id" = "id"))

title: Limitations & Notes
text: selma_efts_report() produces pro-rata approximation EFTS distributing component’s EFTS across calendar months proportionally duration. useful internal planning exactly match official TEC figures. Key reasons: Pro-rata approximation — EFTS allocated proportionally calendar days. official TEC calculation may use different rules partial months, start/end boundaries, rounding. Funding category mapping — package uses compenrsource compenrfundingcategory fields -SELMA. miscategorised SELMA, report inherits errors. Cross-credit detection — Enrolments funded components sum zero EFTS excluded cross-credits. heuristic may catch cases. Withdrawal date handling — Withdrawn enrolments (WR, WS) included using original component dates. actual funded period may differ depending withdrawal processed. official reporting, always validate SELMA’s built-reports TEC guidance.

title: Limitations & Notes
text: constraints SELMA REST API , selmaR package: created/modified dates components — enrolment_components endpoint include createddate updateddate fields, making delta/incremental sync impossible components. must fetch full dataset time. bulk delta sync — way request “records changed since date X” endpoint. Every fetch pulls full dataset. webhooks — SELMA push change notifications. must poll API schedule. Non-paginated intake enrolments — /app/intake_enrolments endpoint returns entire response single JSON payload (Hydra pagination). intakes many enrolments, can large response. Inconsistent ID naming — Different endpoints use different names concept (e.g. id vs intakeid vs progid). selmaR normalises clean_names() original naming source confusion. Undocumented rate limits — API publish rate limit headers. experience 429 503 errors, increase delay requests. Token expiry — Bearer tokens expire without warning. get 401 error mid-fetch, re-authenticate selma_connect().

title: Limitations & Notes
text: Use caching — Set cache = TRUE fetch functions avoid re-downloading data interactive analysis sessions. Schedule fetches — Run nightly hourly script refreshes cached data, analysis always works recent snapshot. Validate EFTS — Cross-check selma_efts_report() output SELMA’s built-funding report least per quarter.

title: MCP Server — Security Design & Implementation Plan
text: document describes design MCP (Model Context Protocol) server exposes selmaR data Claude. server JSON-RPC 2.0 stdio process — reads JSON requests stdin, dispatches tool handlers, writes JSON responses stdout. internal diagnostics go stderr avoid corrupting protocol stream. Six defence layers protect PII responses: code inspection, input allowlisting, configurable field policy, hybrid data access controls, output scanning, audit logging. Deployment model: single user, local, trust boundary R console.

code: .mcp_seed <- as.character(sample.int(1e9, 1)) pseudonymise_id <- function(id, prefix = "S") {   if (is.na(id) || id == "") return(id)   hash <- substr(digest::digest(paste0(.mcp_seed, id), algo = "md5"), 1, 8)   paste0(prefix, "-", hash) }

title: MCP Server — Security Design & Implementation Plan
text: R code evaluated, server parses Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) using parse() walks tree detect blocked constructs. match found, code rejected without execution warning returned client. AST analysis strictly better string matching — ignores comments string literals (false positives # use selmaR::) catches obfuscation attempts like get("selma_request"). Blocked namespace packages (via :: :::): Blocked function calls: Design rationale: none functions needed MCP analysis sessions. workspace provides dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, lubridate, scales, selma helper functions. legitimate analysis patterns (filtering, grouping, summarising, joining, charting) covered tidyverse methods without needing base metaprogramming /O functions. code blocked, server returns MCP error result: blocked attempt recorded audit log full list detected constructs.
code: BLOCKED_PACKAGES <- c("selmaR", "httr", "httr2", "curl", "jsonlite", "config")  BLOCKED_FUNCTIONS <- c(   # Metaprogramming / indirect dispatch   "eval", "evalq", "do.call", "get", "mget", "exists",   "match.fun", "getExportedValue", "loadNamespace", "requireNamespace",   # Environment / credential access   "Sys.getenv", "Sys.setenv",   # Shell execution   "system", "system2", "shell",   # File I/O   "readLines", "scan", "file", "readRDS", "writeLines",   "write.csv", "write.csv2", "saveRDS",   # Network   "download.file", "url", "socketConnection" )  check_code_safety <- function(code) {   expr <- tryCatch(parse(text = code), error = function(e) NULL)   if (is.null(expr)) return(list(safe = TRUE))  # syntax error — will fail at eval    blocked <- character(0)    walk <- function(node) {     if (is.call(node)) {       fn <- node[[1]]       # Detect :: and ::: with blocked packages       if (is.call(fn) && as.character(fn[[1]]) %in% c("::", ":::")) {         pkg <- as.character(fn[[2]])         if (pkg %in% BLOCKED_PACKAGES) {           blocked <<- c(blocked, paste0(pkg, "::", as.character(fn[[3]])))         }       }       # Detect blocked function calls       fn_name <- if (is.symbol(fn)) as.character(fn) else ""       if (fn_name %in% BLOCKED_FUNCTIONS) {         blocked <<- c(blocked, fn_name)       }     }     if (is.recursive(node)) {       for (child in as.list(node)) walk(child)     }   }    for (e in as.list(expr)) walk(e)    if (length(blocked) > 0) {     list(safe = FALSE, blocked = blocked)   } else {     list(safe = TRUE)   } } [BLOCKED] Your code was rejected before execution. Blocked constructs found: selmaR::selma_students, get Reason: These functions bypass PII controls or access restricted resources. Use the workspace functions instead (e.g. selma_students(), selma_get_entity()).

code: apply_field_policy <- function(df, entity_name) {   policy <- .mcp_policy[[entity_name]]   if (is.null(policy) || identical(policy$mode, "all")) return(df)    if (identical(policy$mode, "allow")) {     return(df[, intersect(names(df), policy$fields), drop = FALSE])   }    if (identical(policy$mode, "redact")) {     for (col in intersect(names(df), policy$fields)) df[[col]] <- "[REDACTED]"     return(df)   }    df }

Entity aliases (selma_students() etc.) workspace shadow real package functions, always applying field policy first. Join helpers exposed — operate already-filtered data.

title: MCP Server — Security Design & Implementation Plan
text: server access full unfiltered data field policy applied. entity PII fields first fetched, server extracts unique values known PII columns stores internal dictionary. dictionary never exposed workspace. dictionary scan uses case-insensitive whole-word matching minimise false positives: catches regex can’t: student named “Kowalczyk” appears free-text note field computed output string, regex pattern match — dictionary scan catches “Kowalczyk” harvested surname column. False positive mitigation: Minimum 3-character length threshold — filters values like “Jo”, “Li” Whole-word boundary matching — “Lee” won’t match “employee” “Leeds” Case-insensitive — catches “SMITH” even source “Smith” Dictionary built lazily (entities fetched) doesn’t slow startup redactions (regex dictionary) logged audit trail.
code: # Columns to harvest PII values from (per entity) PII_DICTIONARY_SOURCES <- list(   students = c("surname", "forename", "preferredname",                "email1", "email2", "mobilephone", "homephone",                "workphone", "nsn"),   contacts = c("surname", "forename", "email",                "mobilephone", "workphone") )  # Built during entity fetch, before field policy is applied build_pii_dictionary <- function(df, entity_name) {   cols <- PII_DICTIONARY_SOURCES[[entity_name]]   if (is.null(cols)) return(invisible(NULL))    for (col in intersect(cols, names(df))) {     values <- unique(na.omit(as.character(df[[col]])))     # Filter out short/common values to avoid false positives     values <- values[nchar(values) >= 3]     .pii_dictionary$values <- unique(c(.pii_dictionary$values, values))   } } scan_for_dictionary_pii <- function(text) {   redacted <- text   detections <- 0L    for (val in .pii_dictionary$values) {     pattern <- paste0("\\b", escape_regex(val), "\\b")     if (grepl(pattern, redacted, ignore.case = TRUE)) {       redacted <- gsub(pattern, "[REDACTED:pii]", redacted, ignore.case = TRUE)       detections <- detections + 1L     }   }    list(text = redacted, n_dictionary_hits = detections) }

title: MCP Server — Security Design & Implementation Plan
text: MCP server shuts (stdin EOF / client disconnect), automatically generates human-readable HTML summary {output_dir}/selma_mcp_session_{timestamp}.html writes path stderr. ensures readable audit artifact always available, even user forgets check. report contains: Example shutdown log: Implementation: main loop calls exit:
code: [selmaR] Session summary written to: /path/to/output/selma_mcp_session_20260314_101530.html [selmaR] 12 tool calls | 4 entities accessed | 0 blocked | 2 redactions generate_session_report <- function() {   log_entries <- jsonlite::stream_in(file(.audit$log_path), verbose = FALSE)    # Compute summary stats   n_calls <- nrow(log_entries)   n_blocked <- sum(log_entries$code_blocked %in% TRUE)   n_errors <- sum(log_entries$is_error %in% TRUE)   entities_accessed <- unique(log_entries$entity[!is.na(log_entries$entity)])   all_redactions <- unlist(log_entries$redactions_applied)   duration <- difftime(Sys.time(), .audit$session_start, units = "mins")    # Build HTML report   html <- build_session_html(     session_start = .audit$session_start,     duration = duration,     log_entries = log_entries,     n_calls = n_calls,     n_blocked = n_blocked,     n_errors = n_errors,     entities_accessed = entities_accessed,     redactions = all_redactions,     real_ids_enabled = .mcp_config$expose_real_ids   )    report_path <- file.path(     .audit$output_dir,     paste0("selma_mcp_session_",            format(.audit$session_start, "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S"),            ".html")   )   writeLines(html, report_path)   mcp_log("Session summary written to: ", report_path)   mcp_log(n_calls, " tool calls | ",           length(entities_accessed), " entities accessed | ",           n_blocked, " blocked | ",           length(all_redactions), " redactions") } on.exit(generate_session_report(), add = TRUE)  repeat {   line <- readLines(stdin_con, n = 1)   if (length(line) == 0) break   # ... dispatch ... }

title: MCP Server — Security Design & Implementation Plan
text: combination layers provides defence--depth prompt injection attempting exfiltrate data: ID pseudonymisation — Student IDs NSNs hashed session-scoped seed data reaches Claude. Even layers fail, real IDs never exposed unless explicitly opted via config.yml. Code inspection (AST) — execute_r evaluates anything, code parsed AST walked. Blocked namespace access (selmaR::, httr::, etc.), metaprogramming (eval, .call, get), file /O, network access, shell commands detected rejected. code never runs. Input allowlisting — Claude call arbitrary API endpoints; 7 defined tools available. Field policy — Even Claude writes selma_students() execute_r, workspace wrapper strips PII fields data reaches Claude. Output scanning — PII somehow enters response (e.g. via free-text note field wasn’t policy-filtered), two scanners catch : regex patterns emails, phones, DOBs, plus session-specific PII dictionary built actual student names, NSNs, contact details. Audit log — tool calls, redactions, blocked attempts, real-ID opt-ins logged, injection attempts detectable post-hoc review. Server instructions — Tell Claude never return raw unfiltered data never use :: namespace access, reducing surface area social engineering. Known limitations: AST guard blocks eval, .call, get, metaprogramming functions, closes main bypass routes. However, R dynamic language — sufficiently creative code theoretically construct function references via mechanisms yet blocked (e.g. environment manipulation). output scanner field policy provide additional defence lines. multi-user deployment, process-level isolation needed (scope). output scanner uses regex heuristics — catch possible PII (e.g. free-text names aren’t recognisable pattern). field policy primary defence; scanner safety net.

title: Phone Number Normalisation
text: SELMA stores phone numbers various formats. standardize_phone() uses dialvalidator package (powered Google’s libphonenumber metadata) parse, validate, format phone numbers E.164 international format. default, numbers without country code tried NZ first, AU. handles common case SELMA instances students across countries.

title: Phone Number Normalisation
code: library(selmaR)  standardize_phone("021 123 4567")     # NZ mobile with leading 0 #> [1] "+64211234567" #> "+64211234567"  standardize_phone("0211234567")       # No spaces #> [1] "+64211234567" #> "+64211234567"  standardize_phone("+64211234567")     # Already international #> [1] "+64211234567" #> "+64211234567"  standardize_phone("211234567")        # Mobile without leading 0 #> [1] "+64211234567" #> "+64211234567"

title: Phone Number Normalisation
text: AU numbers +61 prefix work directly. Local AU numbers (e.g. 04xx) automatically detected via NZ--AU fallback — 04 valid NZ prefix, dialvalidator correctly parses Australian:
code: standardize_phone("+61412345678")     # Already international #> [1] "+61412345678" #> "+61412345678"  standardize_phone("0412345678")       # AU local — detected automatically #> [1] "+61412345678" #> "+61412345678"

title: Phone Number Normalisation
text: Numbers fail validation regions returned NA:
code: standardize_phone("1234")            # Too short #> [1] NA #> NA  standardize_phone(NA)                # NA passthrough #> [1] NA #> NA  standardize_phone("")                # Empty string #> [1] NA #> NA

title: Phone Number Normalisation
text: Use standardize_phones() vectors — handles mixed NZ AU numbers:

title: Phone Number Normalisation
code: library(dplyr)  students |>   mutate(     mobile_std = standardize_phone(mobilephone),     secondary_std = standardize_phone(secondaryphone)   )

title: Phone Number Normalisation
text: Override default fallback regions student population differs:
code: # Only try AU (no NZ fallback) standardize_phones(phones, default_regions = "AU")  # Try NZ, AU, then UK standardize_phones(phones, default_regions = c("NZ", "AU", "GB"))

title: Connect to the SELMA API — selma_connect
text: Credentials resolved order: Direct arguments — base_url, email, password config.yml — version-specific block (selma.v2 selma.v3) api_version set, flat selma block fallback Environment variables — version-specific (SELMA_V3_EMAIL etc.) generic (SELMA_EMAIL etc.)

title: Connect to the SELMA API — selma_connect
text: Create config.yml project root (add .gitignore!). Single version (flat structure, backward-compatible):   Dual version (v2 v3 credentials stored separately):   api_version = "v3" set (auto-detected), selmaR reads selma.v3.email / selma.v3.password first, falling back flat selma.email / selma.password present.
code: default:   selma:     base_url: "https://myorg.selma.co.nz/"     email: "api@selma.co.nz"     password: "secret" default:   selma:     v2:       base_url: "https://myorg.selma.co.nz/"       email: "v2_api@selma.co.nz"       password: "v2secret"     v3:       base_url: "https://myorg.selma.app/"       email: "v3_api@selma.app"       password: "v3secret"

what: MCP Server — Ask Claude About Your SELMA Data — selma_mcp
title: MCP Server — Ask Claude About Your SELMA Data — selma_mcp
text: selmaR includes Model Context Protocol (MCP) server lets Claude query SELMA instance natural language. server runs local JSON-RPC 2.0 stdio process exposes 7 tools exploring student management data.

title: MCP Server — Ask Claude About Your SELMA Data — selma_mcp
text: Add server Claude Code settings (~/.claude/settings.json project .claude/settings.json):   server reads credentials config.yml working directory (format selma_connect()).
code: {   "mcpServers": {     "selmaR": {       "command": "Rscript",       "args": ["<path-to-selmaR>/inst/mcp/server.R"],       "cwd": "<directory-containing-config.yml>"     }   } }

title: MCP Server — Ask Claude About Your SELMA Data — selma_mcp
text: MCP server exposes following tools Claude: auth_status Check connection field policy status. list_entities List available SELMA entity types descriptions. search_entities Search entity types keyword (case-insensitive, matches name description). describe_entity Get per-column summary statistics entity. Returns aggregates — never individual rows. get_entity_summary Get filtered/grouped aggregate statistics. Supports filter_by group_by parameters. get_efts_report Generate EFTS funding report source category given year. execute_r Execute R code sandboxed workspace dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, lubridate, scales. Variables persist calls. Code inspected execution — namespace access /O functions blocked.

title: MCP Server — Ask Claude About Your SELMA Data — selma_mcp
text: Student data sensitive. server enforces: ID pseudonymisation — session-scoped deterministic hashing ID columns. AST code inspection — blocked packages functions execute_r. Input allowlisting — 7 tools exposed; raw API access. PII field policy — configurable YAML allowlist controls columns visible per entity (see inst/mcp/field_policy.yml). Hybrid data access — structured tools return aggregates , never individual rows. Output scanning — regex PII dictionary checks responses leave server. Audit logging — JSONL log every tool call plus session summary report.

title: MCP Server — Ask Claude About Your SELMA Data — selma_mcp
text: MCP server consists : inst/mcp/server.R Main MCP server (~1500 lines). inst/mcp/field_policy.yml Default PII field policy — controls columns Claude can see entity. inst/mcp/test_server.R Test suite security layers.
